ops.title: ship exact: bookmark-name quoting — why %q everywhere is not the fix
ops.body:
Review of the ship auto-rebase feature (2026-07-15) flagged that `"exact:" + target` argv concatenation (bookmark move / git push, in `shipPushJJ`) rejects bookmark names containing spaces/quotes/parens, and suggested quoting. Analysis outcome — deliberately NOT changed:

- Git refnames cannot contain spaces (git rejects them), so the headline case is unreachable for pushable bookmarks; jj fails loudly (pattern parse error) on the rest — fail-fast is preserved.
- The obvious "fix" (`fmt.Sprintf("exact:%q", target)` in argv, matching the revset consts) can REGRESS currently-working names: Go `%q` escapes non-ASCII as `é`-style sequences that jj's string-pattern parser does not accept, so a unicode bookmark (e.g. `café`) that works unquoted today would start failing. The revset consts (`bookmarks(exact:%q)`) share this latent unicode limitation since v0.17.0.
- A real fix needs a jj-string-pattern escaper (quote + escape `"` and `\\` only, no unicode escaping) applied uniformly to both the revset consts and the `exact:` argv sites. Worth doing as its own change if exotic bookmark names ever matter; not as a drive-by inside the auto-rebase feature.

Related accepted fixes from the same review: conflict check widened to `conflicts() & (bookmarks(exact:%q)..@-)::` (rebase -b rewrites sibling descendants, not just `::@`), and a heads guard refusing a missing or conflicted (multi-head) target bookmark before any mutation — a conflicted bookmark otherwise becomes a silent merge destination for `jj rebase --destination`.

Also observed, unchanged: an untracked `--bookmark` target's local bookmark stays stale after `jj git fetch`, so divergence isn't detected and the push fails at `jj git push` (pre-existing loud failure mode).
